Your ability to manage competing priorities while upholding rigorous compliance standards will be key to your success in this dynamic environment. Analyse intricate client ownership structures, beneficial ownership details, and sources of funds or wealth to accurately assess risk levels and recommend appropriate mitigation strategies. Assist in developing, updating, and refining KYC policies, procedures, and risk assessment frameworks so they reflect current regulatory changes and best practices within the industry. Facilitate direct client contact for documentation collection in accordance with KYC policy requirements while providing exceptional customer service and minimising requests through effective use of public sources. Mentor junior KYC analysts by sharing best practices, delivering updates on regulatory changes, and guiding them through internal procedures across both onshore and offshore teams. Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams such as legal, operations, and front office departments to streamline onboarding processes and KYC refresh cycles without compromising compliance standards. Maintain accurate updates for all KYC processes using centralised trackers; proactively identify potential roadblocks and devise solutions to efficiently close out outstanding KYC profiles. Influence both internal stakeholders and external partners—including clients—by fostering open communication channels that support timely completion of KYC requirements. Project Title Manufacturing Cost Analysis & Process Improvement Project Project Description This project provides the intern with the opportunity to develop a strong foundation in manufacturing cost analytics and financial planning within a high-volume production environment. Opportunities for full-time employment may be offered based on performance, analytical capability, ability to manage financial datasets, and contributions to process-improvement initiatives. Project Scope (Learning Opportunities) Planning and Reporting Analytics Conduct monthly reviews and analysis of actual reported process costs and compare them against planned targets. Perform quarterly analyses of projected process costs, including comparisons to previous plans and established benchmarks. Validate monthly cost data to ensure consistency for internal and external benchmarking purposes. Respond to management information requests and participate in special analytical studies. Documentation & Process Improvement Collaborate with senior team members to identify areas for process alignment, simplification, and automation. Document business processes identified during improvement initiatives to build standardized reference materials. Deliverables Monthly and quarterly cost analysis reports with clear insights on cost variances. A set of validated and organized financial datasets for benchmarking. Documented process maps or workflows for selected improvement areas. Recommendations on areas where manufacturing finance processes can be simplified or automated. Save Report this job Job Description Compliance - Global Investment Research – Associate, Hong Kong GLOBAL COMPLIANCE Our division prevents, detects and mitigates compliance, regulatory and reputational risk across the firm and helps to strengthen the firm’s culture of compliance. Compliance accomplishes these through the firm’s enterprise-wide compliance risk management program. As an independent control function and part of the firm’s second line of defence, Compliance assesses the firm’s compliance, regulatory and reputational risk; monitors for compliance with new or amended laws, rules and regulations; designs and implements controls, policies, procedures and training; conducts independent testing; investigates, surveils and monitors for compliance risks and breaches; and leads the firm’s responses to regulatory examinations, audits and inquiries. You'll be part of a team with members from a wide range of academic and professional backgrounds, such as law, accounting, sales, and trading. We look for those who possess sound judgment, curiosity, and are able to adapt to a changing regulatory landscape. Responsibilities And Qualifications Business Supported: Global Investment Research Division (GIR). From macroeconomic forecasts to individual stock analysis, GIR develops tools and insights to help shape investment strategies for clients and the firm. GIR analysts work on client-focused research in the equity, fixed-income, currency, and commodities markets, mining big data that enters markets around the world each day to identify game-changing insights. How You Will Fulfill Your Potential Initiative, creativity and drive, together with the ability to work closely and comfortably with research analysts, are essential attributes of all group members. Because the role requires substantive interaction with research analysts and advising on rules and laws impacting research across jurisdictions in Asia Pacific (including Greater China), candidates with legal, regulatory, compliance or other investigatory experience, who can function in a compliance or legal arena, are being sought. Work with global team members located in New York, Singapore, Shanghai, Tokyo, Sydney, Bengaluru, Dallas and London. Advisory and Compliance Oversight. Providing advice to the GIR business, including senior management, on regulatory and reputational related matters, and acting as a resource on a daily basis for compliance and policy issues. Policies and Procedures. Ensuring that GIR has appropriate policies and procedures in place. Assessing, designing and driving completion of necessary upgrades, updates and new policies. Working closely with Legal and GIR to fulfill requirements. Regulatory Matters. Helping to manage GIR’s responses to requests for information from regulators, keeping apprised of latest regulatory developments as it relates to GIR. Training. Designing, executing and delivering training on policies and procedures. Controls/Surveillance/Testing. Creating controls, rolling out surveillance and monitoring of policies and procedures. Preferred Qualifications SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE WE ARE LOOKING FOR Legal/Regulatory background Fluency in English and Chinese Strong oral and written communication skills; ability to communicate confidently and effectively with a diverse audience Quick learner who can adapt well to a fluid regulatory and business environment Ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously, and to work effectively in a team Organized, detail-oriented, enthusiastic and professional About Goldman Sachs At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers . Market risk management tasks will involve performing PnL and risk data generation and reporting, analysing and monitoring market risk activities undertaken by Global Markets (Trading) and Treasury (ALM). [Liquidity Risk] Prepare monthly and other periodic liquidity risk reports for reporting to management Regular monitoring of liquidity risk indicators, balance sheet, net interest income and stress testing Perform data analysis of APAC balance sheet and collaborate with stakeholders to streamline and standardize balance sheet reporting and contingency funding plan of APAC branches Maintain and improve internal processes and EUCs for funding liquidity Risk Management Adhoc support to prepare data and presentation materials for Asset Liability Management Committee meetings for Singapore and APAC including coordination with stakeholders for committee meetings. [ Product Control ] Prepare daily, monthly and other periodic risk reports; Monitor individual dealer’s position and PL; Monitor market risk greeks such as delta, PV01, gamma, vega and theta etc; Monitor, investigate and report market risk limits and credit limits; Reconcile the data among different systems; Capturing market data and ensuring integrity and accuracy of the data before uploading into front systems for Asia and Oceania region; Perform ad-hoc project UAT in order to improve the processes and operations; and Support regional branch in all product control related matters. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in accounting, business, data science, statistics, quantitative finance, economics No prior experience required. Candidates with relevant internship experience may also be considered. Basic understanding of Balance Sheet, ALM and funding risk management is desirable Good knowledge of banking, money market and derivatives products Experience in using Tableau, Excel with VBA skills, Power Query Analytical mindset to extract meaningful insights and trends from data Prior experience in data visualisation, process streamlining, working with large and complex data sets and ability to conceptualise automated control solutions is preferred Good problem-solving skills to take initiative and handle projects involving multiple stakeholders Good initiative and inter-personal skills, ability to work efficiently in a team and independently. Good communication skills (both verbal & writing) with colleagues in Singapore and regional branches. We regret to inform that only shortlisted applicants will be notified. As a Finance Data Domain Architect Associate within our International Consumer Bank team, you will work closely with platform users and analysts to understand data requirements and functional needs. You will have the opportunity to partner with data engineers to design and implement industry-leading data architecture, test new data models, maintain high-quality documentation, and lead in the development of data pipelines. This role provides a unique opportunity to make a real impact in a collaborative, cross-functional, agile team environment. Our International Consumer Bank team is at the heart of this venture, focused on getting smart ideas into the hands of our customers. We're looking for people who have a curious mindset, thrive in collaborative squads, and are passionate about new technology. By their nature, our people are also solution-oriented, commercially savvy and have a head for fintech. We work in tribes and squads that focus on specific products and projects – and depending on your strengths and interests, you'll have the opportunity to move between them. Job Responsibilities Work closely with platform users and analysts to understand data requirements and functional needs Partner with data engineers to design and implement industry leading data architecture to support a highly scalable, reliable and performant platform Testing new data models and enhancements to ensure they meet end user requirements and expected results Maintain high quality technical and end user documentation to support the data models underpinning our platform solutions Implement, own and execute controls including data validation rules and reconciliations as part of our runbook Lead in the development of data pipelines, ad-hoc data analysis and BI solutions to support external financial reporting Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills Bachelor’s degree in accounting or finance related domains Minimum 2 years’ experience in data analytics, change management or a data driven finance function Detail and control oriented with a good understanding of finance and accounting concepts, ideally a finance domain background Experience as a data architect, business analyst or as an end user driving data platform innovation Ability to work in collaborative, cross-functional, agile teams as well as independently Strong communication skills, both written and verbal Proven SQL coding, ETL pipeline or data warehousing experience Ability to translate problem statements into practical solutions and technical requirements Self-starter and highly self-motivated A passion for applying best practices and driving innovation Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills Retail banking domain experience is advantageous Ability to deliver large scale or ‘big data’ platform solutions Working as part of multi-location team ABOUT US J.P. We’re a tech-forward, people-empowered network with more than 370,000 people in 149 countries. Across audit and assurance, tax and legal, deals and consulting we help clients build, accelerate and sustain momentum. Find out more at www.pwc.com. We are Asia’s leading Asset and Wealth Management practice and our firm is offering you an exceptional opportunity to work with market-leading, fast-growing and specialist boutique clients in this this dynamic sector. AWMS seeks to deliver tailored one-stop-shop solutions that support the daily operations and management needs of fund managers, family offices and funds in today’s complex operating environment, empowered by our team of multi-disciplinary specialists and leading technological solutions. Our AWMS team is expanding rapidly and we are looking for seasoned professionals to join us. A career within AWMS Risk & Compliance Solutions will provide you with the opportunity to help companies rethink their approach to risk and compliance and create a sustainable business advantage. We’re part of a unique client proposition that advises our clients on routine and complex risk and regulatory matters and offers bespoke outsourced support, from market entry through to execution, to help our clients better manage their ongoing compliance, risk and internal audit needs. You’ll work as part of a fast-growing team of subject matter experts with extensive industry experience in areas relating to regulatory compliance, risk management and internal controls. The team’s track record and credentials include several “industry-firsts” and numerous high profile engagements. Your clients will include leading local, regional and global asset managers, alternative asset managers (private equity, private credit, real estate, hedge funds and venture capital), traditional and digital wealth managers, digital assets and other capital market intermediaries. Digital alliances and digital solutions are core to the delivery of our services to clients. You will enjoy a stable yet flexible working environment with certainty in hours, and the ability to manage and control your own time. Specific responsibilities include but are not limited to: Assisting to provide advice to fund management companies, wealth managers, digital advisors, digital assets providers and other capital markets intermediaries (i.e. REIT managers, brokerages, corporate finance advisors and trust companies); to help optimise their governance, risk management and compliance with regulatory requirements Assisting in conducting business processes and controls reviews, including control assurance reporting to identifying gaps, assessing risks and providing recommendations for remediation; Assisting in Reviewing and recommending practical business process improvements to address control gaps and to enhance efficiency where possible; Draft findings in report deliverables with clear and concise business writing abilities; Assisting outsourced compliance support to clients. This includes regulatory reporting, operationalising changes and updates to regulations, conducting compliance monitoring and reporting to senior management, assisting on AML/KYC reviews and providing regulatory advice; About you: Related area Accounting / Commerce / Business including students from a local/overseas university. For candidates with other degree specialisations, preference will be given to candidate that have obtained or are in the process of pursuing Singapore CA, ICAEW, CIA or CISA accreditations. Good understanding of the asset and wealth management/digital assets/capital markets landscape and IT audit experience preferred Knowledge of local MAS regulatory requirements for capital markets intermediaries gained through experience in regulatory compliance, internal audit or processes, risk and controls experience. Experience in the following areas would be beneficial: auditing under the relevant frameworks (for ISAE 3402, ISEA 3000, AUP); and Technology-related regulatory requirements, which includes Technology Risk Management, Cyber Hygiene and Business Continuity Management; and Direct dealings and interactions with the MAS. Good track record of academic achievements Meticulous and attention to details, possessing an analytical and enquiring mind-set Professional, confident and highly motivated self-starter, with an ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast pace environment with tight deadlines, Positive attitude to learn and strong team player. Able to work under pressure and tight deadlines Possess strong values, personal integrity and emotional intelligence. Save Report this job Position Summary We are seeking a highly motivated Business Analyst to join our Finance Controlling team. The role is responsible for managing monthly finance closing activities, performing profit & loss (P&L) and cost analysis, ensuring proper accounting postings in SAP FI/CO, and providing management with actionable insights through in-depth data analysis. The position requires strong Excel and SAP skills, with the ability to prepare accurate and detailed P&L analysis with large dataset. Role And Responsibilities Role Responsibilities: Monthly Finance closing: Manage monthly financial closing in SAP, including sales and expense postings, cost allocations, and reconciliations. Ensure data accuracy and completeness by coordinating with relevant teams and resolving issues before deadlines. P&L and Cost Analysis: Perform detailed monthly profit & loss and cost analysis, identifying key variances versus management target plan and prior month actual. Share P&L analysis excel report with sales/marketing team and adjust future sales / expense forecasting accordingly. Data Analysis & Reporting: Conduct in-depth business and financial analysis using Excel and SAP system data. Develop excel based structured reports to provide management with insights into cost structures, reason for profit issue, expense trends, and improvement opportunities. Variance Analysis: Investigate root causes of cost increase or profitability decreases compared to prior periods and monthly financial analysis reports using advanced Excel techniques (pivot tables, advanced excel formulas) after closing. SAP FI/CO Monitoring: Work closely with FI posting team to ensure correct postings and proper cost allocation across channel & business unit. Review and validate cost allocation in SAP CO system configuration, make necessary adjustment of system configuration. Cross-functional Collaboration: Partner with Sales, Marketing, and other departments to review marketing investment, analyze cost drivers and P&L impact for accurate finance P&L forecasting. Skills And Qualifications Role Requirements: 5-8 years of relevant experience in Finance Controlling, Business Analysis, or Accounting. Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field. Strong knowledge of SAP FI/CO system (FI posting, cost allocation, and system logic). Ability to manage monthly finance closing activities, ensuring accuracy and timelines. Ability to handle and analyse large data sets, and create excel analysis reports on sales/profit/ expenses by channel, model and so on. Solid understanding of accounting principles including provisions, accruals, and expense recognition. Hands-on experiences on finance closing with SAP system Experience in financial system enhancement projects is a plus Professional certification like CPA, ACCA is a plus Advanced Excel skills (advance formula such as SUMIFS/INDEX, pivot). VBA/macro is a plus. Please visit Samsung membership to see Privacy Policy, which defaults according to your location. You can change Country/Language at the bottom of the page. If you are European Economic Resident, please click here. Across the globe, we’re 150,000 colleagues, striving to make a difference for every client, organization, and community we serve. MUFG bank is seeking an Analyst / Associate in Planning Department at Global Markets Division for Asia, based in Hong Kong. This is an exceptional opportunity for you to play a pivotal role in supporting the day-to-day operations and strategic planning of a highly respected business area. You will be part of a supportive team that values your input and encourages professional growth through exposure to regional projects and cross-border collaboration. If you are looking to develop your career within a nurturing environment where your interpersonal skills and commitment to excellence are truly valued, this position provides the perfect platform. Join a globally recognised financial institution with a strong presence across Asia, offering you the chance to work on impactful regional initiatives and collaborate with international teams. Be part of an inclusive workplace that values diversity, fosters open communication, and supports your growth through meaningful responsibilities and stakeholder engagement. What you'll do: As an Analyst / Associate in Planning Department at Global Markets Division for Asia, you will play an integral role in supporting both daily operations and long-term strategic initiatives. Your responsibilities will include coordinating essential business functions such as budgeting, forecasting, and reviewing internal control processes. Building strong relationships with stakeholders—both locally and internationally—will be central to your success in this role. You will also ensure that all activities comply fully with regulatory requirements by upholding ethical standards and assisting in policy development. In addition to regular reporting duties, you will have opportunities to participate in special projects that broaden your experience and contribute meaningfully to the division’s objectives. Coordinate and manage the daily operations of the Global Markets business area, ensuring all activities run smoothly and efficiently while maintaining high standards of accuracy. Support the Planning Head in developing comprehensive business strategies, contributing to business promotion planning, operational efficiency improvements, and the successful implementation of regional projects. Develop and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders both internally across departments and externally with partners, fostering effective communication and collaboration. Partner closely with overseas branches and Head Office teams to support the creation and execution of robust business strategies for the Global Markets Division. Ensure full compliance with prevailing regulations by demonstrating ethical behaviour at all times and adhering strictly to all internal controls established for the team. Assist in developing, updating, and aligning departmental policies with relevant laws and regulations to ensure ongoing compliance across all activities. Take responsibility for preparing management or departmental reports, providing accurate information that supports decision-making processes within the division. Participate actively in ad hoc projects or assignments as required, contributing your insights and expertise to drive successful outcomes. What you bring: To excel the role of an Analyst / Associate in Planning Department at Global Markets Division for Asia, you will bring proven experience from either banking or financial services environments—or demonstrate strong academic credentials if you are early in your career. Your background equips you with practical knowledge of market trends, product offerings, regulatory frameworks, and best practices within financial institutions. You possess excellent organisational capabilities that help you manage complex tasks efficiently while maintaining attention to detail. Your interpersonal skills foster positive relationships with colleagues across different regions and cultures. Effective communication—especially fluency in both Japanese and English—enables you to share ideas clearly whether collaborating on projects or preparing reports. Above all, your proactive attitude ensures that you consistently seek ways to enhance operational effectiveness while upholding ethical standards. A university degree in Banking, Finance or a related discipline provides you with the foundational knowledge necessary for this role. At least 3 years’ relevant experience within banking or financial institutions is preferred; however, candidates who are recent graduates with an academic background in accounting are also encouraged to apply. A solid understanding of industry trends, global markets business practices, financial products, as well as relevant laws and regulations is essential for success. Your maturity enables you to approach tasks thoughtfully while being proactive in identifying areas for improvement within processes or strategies. The ability to work independently ensures you can manage responsibilities effectively while contributing positively within a team setting. Excellent organisational skills allow you to handle multiple priorities efficiently without compromising quality or deadlines. Direct message the job poster from Shopee Jordan Cordell Ong Jordan Cordell Ong Recruiting at Shopee Singapore | Hiring Strategic Talents! 🤩 About the Team: Our Chatbot & Customer Service team is redefining the Shopee platform experience through cutting-edge Conversational AI. We are seeking a Product Analyst who is passionate about leveraging data to shape the future of our chatbot product. In this role, you will go beyond traditional analysis to gain a deep understanding of the machine learning models that power our chatbot, design robust evaluation frameworks, and partner directly with Data Scientists to drive model iteration and product strategy. Your insights will be critical in creating a more intuitive, helpful, and successful shopping assistant for our millions of users. Job Description: Advanced Metrics Design: Architect the key metrics and evaluation frameworks that define success for our chatbot product. Move beyond standard metrics to develop sophisticated measures of user satisfaction, conversation quality, task completion, and long-term business impact (e.g., conversion lift, support ticket deflection). Data-Driven Product Strategy: Translate complex data and model performance into clear, actionable insights for product and engineering teams. Your analysis will directly influence the product roadmap and prioritisation of new features. Cross-Functional DS Collaboration: Work hand-in-hand with Data Scientists, ML Engineers, and Product Managers to formulate hypotheses, design effective A/B tests and multivariate experiments for model and feature launches, and analyse results to provide actionable recommendations. Holistic User Journey & UX Analysis: Conduct comprehensive analyses of the entire user conversation flow. Identify key drop-off points, friction areas, and moments of delight within the chatbot experience. Partner with UX Researchers to triangulate quantitative data with qualitative feedback. Stakeholder Management & Communication: Communicate findings and recommendations effectively to all levels of the organisation, from engineers to senior management. Build and maintain scalable dashboards and reports to provide visibility into chatbot health and performance. Develop Domain Expertise: Become the subject matter expert on marketplace dynamics as they relate to conversational commerce, understanding user behavior, and the business impact of the chatbot. About The Team The Business Intelligence and Data Analytics team is the engine behind our data-driven business iterations. This year, we are evolving our core capabilities by integrating AI into our workflows. We are looking for a forward-thinking Junior BI Analyst to support our AI transformation by helping to bridge the gap between traditional analytics and next-generation intelligence. description: Kerry Consulting hiring Structured Commodity Finance Analyst - Energy Trading Group in Singapore, Singapore | LinkedIn Skip to main content LinkedIn Structured Commodity Finance Analyst - Energy Trading Group in Hong Kong Expand search Jobs This button displays the currently selected search type. When expanded it provides a list of search options that will switch the search inputs to match the current selection. Jobs People Learning Clear text Clear text Clear text Clear text Clear text Sign in Join now Structured Commodity Finance Analyst - Energy Trading Group Kerry Consulting Singapore, Singapore Apply Structured Commodity Finance Analyst - Energy Trading Group Kerry Consulting Singapore, Singapore 13 hours ago Be among the first 25 applicants See who Kerry Consulting has hired for this role Apply Save Report this job Overview A global commodities and energy group with an established international trading platform is expanding its structured finance capabilities to support growing activity in the Asia‑Pacific region. The firm is deeply involved in physical trading and logistics, working closely with banks, financiers and commercial stakeholders to deliver sophisticated financing solutions that underpin trading flows and asset-backed transactions. Role The role sits within the structured finance team, supporting the execution of structured trade, collateralised and shipping‑related financing transactions. You will be responsible for financial and cash flow modelling, collateral analysis, preparation of pitch materials and transaction documentation, and coordination with banks and internal stakeholders. This role offers exposure to live transactions and a broad view of structured commodity finance and corporate funding activities. Requirements The successful candidate has minimum 5 years of relevant experience in structured finance, trade finance, corporate finance, or investment banking, and holds a bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related discipline. A solid understanding of commodities markets, asset‑backed financing and loan documentation is required. You are best described as detail‑oriented, analytical and comfortable managing multiple workstreams under tight deadlines, with excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Mandarin to support regional stakeholders. A proactive mindset and willingness to travel when necessary are expected. To Apply To apply, please submit your resume (in MS Word format) to Keren Woo at keren@kerryconsulting.com, quoting the job title and reference number KW 35737. We regret that only successfully shortlisted applicants will be contacted. Job Description The Payment Product Partnerships Analyst supports the execution of the regional third-party engagement model and governance processes to facilitate American Express (AXP) product and capability adoption across markets in the region and with selected global providers. This role partners closely with cross-functional teams, including local PCG, Product, and Merchant Services, to contribute to product deployments, maintain third-party documentation and controls, and support adherence to enterprise Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM) policies and standards. Responsibilities Regional Third‑Party Support & Execution Support the execution of regional third-party plans and rollout activities across markets and specialized global providers. Assist local teams and partners during product implementations and upgrades by coordinating tasks, tracking progress, and providing structured inputs to support prioritization and delivery decisions. Third‑Party Relationship & Commercial Support Support the management of the regional third-party portfolio by maintaining documentation, preparing analyses, and assisting with service-level monitoring and product readiness activities. Contribute inputs to commercial discussions and product support arrangements to help ensure AXP product availability and quality at the point of sale (POS), under guidance from senior stakeholders. Governance, Risk & Controls Support the implementation and ongoing maintenance of regional third-party governance processes, including controls, documentation standards, and reporting, in alignment with enterprise TPRM requirements. Assist with data collection, issue tracking, and coordination with local markets to support consistent execution. Business Development & Market Insights Contribute to the identification of third-party opportunities and emerging payment trends through research, analysis, and information gathering. Support pipeline tracking and assist in synthesizing industry insights and market intelligence for internal stakeholders. Consultative Delivery & Cross‑Functional Collaboration Provide analytical and operational support to regional initiatives by collaborating with NAS, PCG, GMNS, Product, Risk, GCO, and Technology teams. Contribute to project delivery and provide product and process inputs to support timely execution within defined scope. Qualifications Foundational knowledge of third-party governance, compliance, and risk management frameworks, including exposure to TPRM concepts Strong analytical skills, with the ability to prepare structured inputs, documentation, and recommendations to support decision-making Ability to translate defined objectives into well-organized execution tasks and follow-up actions Effective written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to collaborate across teams and support stakeholder interactions Strong organizational skills, including the ability to track actions, manage dependencies, and support project delivery activities Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to facilitate cross-functional collaboration and influence stakeholders Experience gathering, synthesizing, and communicating industry or partner insights to internal stakeholders About Us At American Express, our culture is built on a 175-year history of innovation, shared values and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. From delivering differentiated products to providing world-class customer service, we operate with a strong risk mindset, ensuring we continue to uphold our brand promise of trust, security, and service. As part of Team Amex, you’ll experience our powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career. Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express. About The Team We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally: Competitive base salaries Bonus incentives Support for financial-well-being and retirement Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location) Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location) Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location) Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program Career development and training opportunities Offer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations. Save Report this job Do you want your voice heard and your actions to count? Discover your opportunity with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s leading financial groups. Across the globe, we’re 150,000 colleagues, striving to make a difference for every client, organization, and community we serve. We stand for our values, building long-term relationships, serving society, and fostering shared and sustainable growth for a better world. With a vision to be the world’s most trusted financial group, it’s part of our culture to put people first, listen to new and diverse ideas and collaborate toward greater innovation, speed and agility. This means investing in talent, technologies, and tools that empower you to own your career. Join MUFG, where being inspired is expected and making a meaningful impact is rewarded. This position is responsible for monitoring exposures and collateral value to ensure financings remain within approved limits, and facility structures/conditions, maintaining, creating and designing all necessary controls and execute administrative duties required for facility and day-to-day execution of client’s request by working closely with other relevant Operations Departments. Job Descriptions: Respond to clients’ requests and coordinate with the relevant Operations Depts for the prompt execution of transactions on daily basis; Respond to clients’ export LC discount requirements by liaising with Tokyo on the granting of FI limits; Liaise with clients daily to follow up on all relevant transactions for proper monitoring/management within credit approvals; Respond to clients’ ad-hoc requests and at times, special requests, which may require discussions with the various depts. within the bank to meet their requirements; Process all respective client requests submitted under approved facilities in accordance with all approvals and conditions; Administer, monitor and control on a daily basis the Commodity Trade Finance (“CTF”) clients’ utilizations and exposures under the facility portfolio. This will ensure correct determination of facility availability at time of new requests or enquiries, and allow decisions to be made that may be proceeded or not; Administer the credit limit setup of Commodity Structured Finance (“CSF”) accounts. Given a portfolio of accounts (CTF/CSF/JC/GSB) to handle, to be familiar with individual processes and business flows for each different client; taking an ownership view; Ensure proper/full legal documentation in place before establishing of credit facilities in bank system; Handles the receiving/safekeeping and releasing of security trade documents on daily basis (within division procedures); Ensure credit approval conditions are adhered to by maintaining updated and accurate records in the Transaction Management Tool, “Yojin”; Monitor balances between Bank’s OVS system and “Yojin” or all allocated or monitored limits in order to retain an accurate record of exposure; Ensure compliance with relevant regulations and limits; Maintain good working knowledge of industry trends, products, relevant laws and regulations; Adhere to all company policies / guidelines. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in finance or accounting or business or similar field. Minimum 3 years’ experience in Commodity Trade Finance or Structured Trade Finance Operations (Back Office) or Collateral Management (Middle Office) functions from banking or commodity trading houses Experience working in middle or back-office functions within Commodity Trade Finance Good interpersonal and communication skills, team-player, ability to adapt to changing needs of the organization and demands of internal and external stakeholders Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG) is an equal opportunity employer. We view our employees as our key assets as they are fundamental to our long-term growth and success. MUFG is committed to hiring based on merit and organsational fit, regardless of race, religion or gender. Company Overview Vantage is a globally recognized multi-asset brokerage firm headquartered in Sydney, operating under the oversight of reputable regulatory authorities. With a workforce of over 1,000 employees spread across more than 30 offices worldwide, we bring over 15 years of industry experience to our clients. We offer trading opportunities in CFDs for Forex, Commodities, Indices, Shares, and Cryptocurrencies. Our commitment is to provide a robust trading environment, complemented by a fast and user-friendly trading platform, enabling our clients to capitalize on trading opportunities efficiently. To learn more about our company, please visit our website: https://www.vantagemarkets.com/ Employment Type: Hybrid / Remote Position Summary We are seeking a highly strategic and execution-oriented Senior Manager to serve as a pivotal force multiplier for our APAC regional leadership. This role is the central hub for translating strategy into action, driving cross-market alignment, and ensuring the delivery of critical regional initiatives. More than an advisor or an administrator, you will be a key operator who structures chaos, manages complex workflows, and drives tangible outcomes across a fast-growing, multi-country FinTech environment. This position is based in Hong Kong with a primary focus on APAC. Key Responsibilities: Regional Management & Operational Coordination: Act as a chief of staff and execution partner to the Regional Head, optimizing management processes, meeting cadences, and operational rhythms. Own key management workflows, including preparation for leadership meetings, documentation of decisions, tracking of action items, and ensuring seamless coordination between HQ, regional, and country teams. Foster cross-market collaboration by facilitating the sharing of best practices, aligning resources, and developing standardized operating procedures (SOPs). Strategic Initiative & Project Leadership: Lead and drive high-priority regional projects from conception to completion, such as market entry/expansion, major offline events, and key regulatory implementations. Break down strategic objectives into executable workstreams, align stakeholders across business, operations, and compliance functions, and ensure on-time, high-quality delivery. Serve as the central point of accountability for cross-functional project coordination, removing bottlenecks and ensuring strategic alignment. Business Intelligence & Performance Analysis: Consolidate regional business data into clear, actionable dashboards and management reports to support data-driven decision-making. Conduct in-depth business and performance analysis to identify growth opportunities, operational risks, and areas for improvement. Monitor industry trends, competitor activity, and regulatory developments, providing structured insights to inform strategic and operational adjustments. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or a related field; an MBA or other advanced degree is strongly preferred. Minimum of 8-10 years of progressive professional experience, with at least 3 years in a relevant role such as Management Consulting (top-tier), Chief of Staff, Corporate Strategy, or Regional/Business Operations within a high-growth environment. Proven experience in the FinTech, financial services, or technology sectors, with exposure to commercial operations, sales management, or growth initiatives. Demonstrated track record of leading complex, cross-functional or cross-market projects to successful completion. Exceptional stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate effectively across cultures and seniority levels. Full professional fluency in English and Mandarin (both written and spoken) is mandatory. Fluency in Cantonese is a significant advantage. Competencies Highly structured and analytical, with a relentless focus on execution and results. Superior communication and presentation skills, capable of distilling complexity into clear narratives for leadership. Agile and resilient, comfortable operating with autonomy in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Proactive problem-solver with strong business acumen and strategic thinking. High level of integrity and discretion when handling sensitive information. What We Offer A high-impact, high-visibility role at the strategic center of a leading global FinTech platform’s growth in APAC. The opportunity to work directly with senior regional leadership and shape operational excellence across multiple markets. A dynamic, challenging, and intellectually stimulating work environment. Competitive executive compensation package, including performance-based bonuses and long-term incentives. Job Description Your Missions: Manage and track daily production problems and user requests, ensuring effective and timely resolution and clear communication with clients. Interact regularly with business users, development, and infrastructure teams on system-related issues and enhancement requests. Maintain high availability of systems by continuously monitoring operations and performance. Conduct pricing performance analysis and provide key risk and performance indicators for capacity planning and strategic decision-making. Perform functional and technical analysis of business requirements for flow and structured product systems enhancements and new feature developments. Document findings, analysis, requirements, workflows, and technical designs for IT development. Collaborate closely with regional and global development teams to ensure smooth delivery of functional and technical requirements. Conduct system Quality Assurance Testing and assist in User Acceptance Testing. Perform production deployment of new versions for business-critical applications. Follow ITIL Application Support methodology to carry out incident and system change management procedures. Participate in support roster, including shift duty, weekend, and holiday work as required. Your Profile: Graduate or postgraduate, preferably with a major in IT or Finance. Experience in capital markets, equities, and derivatives products. Experience with process automation using Python and Shell scripting. Proficient in SQL. Knowledge of Excel/VBA and Java is desirable. Experience working with Linux and Windows operating systems. Proactive, meticulous, reliable, and results-oriented team player with a passion for self-driven learning. Experience in a fast-paced trading floor environment is an advantage. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to anticipate business evolution. Experience with system monitoring tools such as Dynatrace, OpenSearch, Grafana, and Prometheus. English fluent. What We Offer An international community bringing together 110+ different nationalities An environment where trust has a central place: 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilities A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modules A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.) Strong commitments to CSR, notably through participation in our WeCare Together program Diversity and Inclusion Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics. Particular attention will be paid to candidates with disabilities. Who are we? Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1,000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success. At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like: Brief Call : Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you! Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities! Case study : Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc. As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience. We look forward to meeting you! Save Report this job Do you want your voice heard and your actions to count? Discover your opportunity with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s leading financial groups. Across the globe, we’re 150,000 colleagues, striving to make a difference for every client, organization, and community we serve. We stand for our values, building long-term relationships, serving society, and fostering shared and sustainable growth for a better world. With a vision to be the world’s most trusted financial group, it’s part of our culture to put people first, listen to new and diverse ideas and collaborate toward greater innovation, speed and agility. This means investing in talent, technologies, and tools that empower you to own your career. Join MUFG, where being inspired is expected and making a meaningful impact is rewarded. Corporate Advisory Office Corporate Advisory Intern Internship Period: 3 August 2026 to 31 December 2026 This position is with the Corporate Advisory Office (CAO), which provide our clients with corporate finance and strategic advice in relation to mergers and acquisitions (“M&A”) in the Asia and Oceania region. Job Description Support Corporate Advisory Office to achieve smooth execution of M&A projects through research and analysis of data. Assist in deal origination by proactively supporting the team in various tasks. This includes maintenance of the team database, conducting financial analysis and building financial model for the purpose of valuation. Assist in preparing good presentation materials for the purpose of deal origination and deal execution. Job Requirements Pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Economics or a related field Self-motivated team player who has a keen interest in financial analysis and corporate finance Able to deliver work under demanding timelines in a fast moving environment Strong communication and interpersonal skills Possess advanced/intermediate proficiency in MS Office (PowerPoint & Excel) Good knowledge of research tools such as CAP IQ, MergerMarket, SPEEDA and Bloomberg will be advantageous IMPORTANT NOTE Interested applicants will be required to submit CV and Cover Letter Please submit your details via our early careers website at Careers (myworkdayjobs.com) Do note that only shortlisted applicants will be notified This internship role is based in Singapore. It is open to Singaporean and PR students only. In Singapore, MUFG internship partners include National University of Singapore, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Management University, Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD) and Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS). Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG) is an equal opportunity employer. We view our employees as our key assets as they are fundamental to our long-term growth and success. MUFG is committed to hiring based on merit and organsational fit, regardless of race, religion or gender. We can sponsor visas for qualified candidates!*** About Linjer Founded in 2014, Linjer is a design studio with Scandinavian roots dedicated to creating timeless, high-quality jewelry, bags, and watches. Frustrated by the limited options between fast fashion and overpriced luxury, we partner directly with top suppliers to deliver beautiful, well-crafted products at a fraction of traditional luxury prices. We are committed to thoughtful design and sustainable practices, using recycled metals, ethically sourced gemstones, and conflict-free, lab-grown diamonds. Our company is rapidly scaling, and we’re excited to expand our Hong Kong office by adding new team members this year! What Linjer Offers A multicultural and inclusive team culture Talented and passionate colleagues committed to excellence Work/life balance with a predictable 9am-6pm work schedule The opportunity to make an impact with your work from day one Exposure to entrepreneurship in a stable business growing 100%+ every year Accelerated career growth opportunities for high achievers Visa sponsorship for exceptional talent ready to join our family Who we’re looking for We’re a fast-growing direct-to-consumer (DTC) brand looking for a sharp Finance Analyst at the start of their career. This role is ideal for someone with outstanding analytical ability, strong business intuition, and the curiosity to use modern tools, including AI, to solve real business problems. You may be a fresh graduate or have 1–2 years of experience, but you think in models, question assumptions, and enjoy solving complex problems with numbers and data. Responsibilities Build and maintain financial models to support pricing, forecasting, and inventory planning Analyze sales, marketing spend, and customer behavior to uncover actionable insights Track key metrics such as CAC, LTV, contribution margin, and cohort performance Support budgeting, forecasting, and monthly performance reviews Own payment and revenue reconciliation across platforms such as Shopify and payment gateways Review and release payments, ensuring every payment is accurate and approved before sending Investigate discrepancies in transactions, payouts, refunds, and chargebacks Serve as the main internal point of contact for maintaining accurate and up-to-date accounting records with outsourced bookkeeping partners Review, organize, and validate bookkeeping outputs to ensure completeness, consistency, and accuracy Work closely with operations and procurement to ensure clean financial reporting Identify process gaps in billing, payments, reconciliation, and accounting workflows, and help design scalable solutions Use AI tools to speed up data analysis, surface anomalies, draft reports, and improve workflow efficiency Who you are Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills Strong proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets; bonus if you know SQL or Python Degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, Mathematics, or a similarly quantitative field Ability to structure ambiguous problems and communicate insights clearly High attention to detail with a bias toward accuracy and rigor Curiosity about how a DTC business operates end-to-end Comfortable using AI tools as part of daily work Bonus Points Familiarity with reconciliation processes, payouts, bookkeeping, and financial operations workflows Internship experience in finance, consulting, analytics, or startups Experience using AI tools for analysis, reporting, or automation Why Join Us High ownership from day one Steep learning curve across finance, marketing, operations, and accounting Opportunity to grow quickly with the business Smart colleagues and a warm work culture If you enjoy thinking in numbers and solving messy real-world problems, we’d love to hear from you! If you have never received training in writing a cover letter before, please search online for guidelines on how to write a cover letter. We take cover letters very seriously! The role involves liaising with stakeholders to gain an understanding of processes and operations, and providing solutions tailored to business needs. Primary Responsibilities Understand, document, and analyze business flows, systems, and data to capture current state and generate solution options. Lead and facilitate discussions with business users and development teams to gather user requirements, user stories, and develop backlogs aligned with business objectives. Develop detailed technical requirements and system changes with the development team, and track deliverables to ensure business needs are met. Prepare documentation including process flows, requirements, user guides, support documents, and interface specifications. Assist product owners in assessing and analyzing options to support business decisions. Collaborate closely with business users across multiple regions, development & QA teams (onshore & offshore), and global IT support teams to facilitate user testing exercises. Develop UAT test plans and smoke test cases to ensure enhancements and changes are validated. Key Performance Indicators Clear definition of business problems and processes. Delivery of solutions that meet user requirements. Qualifications Experience Minimum of 3 years’ experience in Business Analyst functions or equivalent. Familiarity with Agile Development Methodology. Education Bachelor’s Degree in IT, Business Systems, or equivalent. Skills / Competencies Strong knowledge of modeling techniques, methods, and tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Visio, PowerPoint. Excellent problem analysis and solving skills. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Good understanding of the software development life cycle. Solid knowledge of general IT concepts. Familiarity with industries such as Gaming, Hotel, and Retail is an advantage. Strong team player, proactive, and self-starting. As part of the creation of a new Asia cluster based in Singapore, we are looking for an Analyst – Asia Retail & Trade to join this newly established team. Reporting to the Sales Operations Manager, the role is responsible for analyzing the performance of the Retail channel and its store network across Singapore, Japan, Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan, providing data‑driven insights to influence decision‑making and drive commercial improvements. About The Role The role leads day‑to‑day and post‑performance reporting for the local Retail Store business, including KPI analysis, store benchmarking, and the creation of reports to empower teams with data. Additionally, the role includes responsibility for revenue forecasting and analysis of retail‑related promotions, products, and productivity, as well as supporting ad‑hoc analytical requests. The Business Analyst acts as a key support partner to Country Sales Heads and retail leadership, adapting reporting and insights to different levels of retail and data maturity across Asia markets, and ensuring data is translated into clear, practical, and actionable conclusions. You will foster an inclusive, engaging, and high-performing culture, embedding Pandora’s core values— Dream, Dare, Care, and Deliver —in everything we do. Key Responsibilities KPI Monitoring & Performance Reporting Conduct day‑to‑day, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly reporting of key retail trading KPIs at country, market, store, and transactional level Prepare and share performance reporting with Sales Management, Country Sales Heads, and other stakeholders, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and consistency Support post‑performance analysis by highlighting trends, deviations, and performance versus targets or prior periods Commercial Insights & Performance Commentary Provide clear, concise, and credible commercial insights for regular performance reviews and local business discussions Analyze performance patterns, identify root causes, and translate data into actionable insights and recommendations Create a strong feedback loop by combining qualitative input from retail and sales teams with quantitative performance data to formulate meaningful commentary Revenue Forecasting Support revenue forecasting at store‑ and daily‑level across Asia markets Work closely with Retail teams, Finance, and Merchandising to gather inputs, validate assumptions, and continuously refine forecasting models Monitor forecast versus actuals and support variance analysis to improve forecasting accuracy Promotions, Product & Productivity Analysis Analyze the performance of retail promotions, campaigns, and initiatives to evaluate effectiveness and impact Support product‑ and assortment‑level analysis to understand performance differences across stores and markets Conduct productivity analysis (e.g., per store, per staff member, per hour) to identify operational improvement opportunities Ad‑hoc Analysis & Cross‑Functional Collaboration Collaborate closely with the Retail Operations Manager, Sales Management, and Country Sales Heads, as well as cross‑functional teams such as Training, Merchandising, eCommerce, Marketing, and Finance Support and prioritize ad‑hoc analysis requests to uncover growth opportunities and support commercial decision‑making Reporting Enablement & BI Collaboration Collaborate with Global and Regional partners to refine and continuously improve globally maintained BI tools and dashboards for effective usage by non‑Finance stakeholders Act as a connector between Global BI teams and Asia Retail teams, ensuring local reporting needs and market realities are reflected Support effective adoption and usage of reporting outputs by local stakeholders Best‑Practice Sharing Partner with Regional and Country Sales Teams to identify best‑in‑class as well as underperforming stores Share best practices, insights, and performance learnings across markets to help boost retail performance at all levels Ideal Profile Experience & Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Analytics, or a related discipline Minimum 3–6 years’ experience in a Business Analyst, Finance Analyst, Commercial Analyst, or similar analytical role Prior exposure to retail, consumer, or multi‑store environments is strongly preferred Experience supporting regional or multi‑market stakeholders, ideally within Asia Hands‑on experience with performance reporting, forecasting, and KPI‑based analysis Skills & Capabilities Strong analytical and numerical skills, with the ability to interpret complex data sets and trends Solid understanding of retail KPIs, store economics, and performance drivers Advanced proficiency in Excel; experience with reporting tools and dashboards is an advantage Strong ability to translate data into clear insights and practical recommendations Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messages to different audiences High attention to detail combined with the ability to see the broader commercial picture Strong time‑management and prioritization skills in a fast‑paced, evolving environment Personal Attributes Commercially minded and curious, with a strong desire to understand business drivers Structured, logical thinker with a problem‑solving mindset Confident communicator who can challenge constructively and influence through insights Collaborative team player, comfortable working across cultures and markets Proactive, self‑motivated, and comfortable operating in ambiguity High level of integrity, accountability, and ownership Why join Pandora in Asia? Jobs People Learning Clear text Clear text Clear text Clear text Clear text Sign in Join now IT Business Analyst Crédit Agricole CIB Singapore, Singapore Apply IT Business Analyst Crédit Agricole CIB Singapore, Singapore 17 hours ago Over 200 applicants See who Crédit Agricole CIB has hired for this role Apply Save Report this job Direct message the job poster from Crédit Agricole CIB Deepa Menon Deepa Menon Technology Recruitment Specialist Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Banking (Crédit Agricole CIB) is the corporate and investment banking arm of Crédit Agricole Group, world’s 10th largest bank by total assets. Our Singapore center is the 2nd largest IT setup (after Paris Head Office) for Crédit Agricole CIB's worldwide business. We work daily with international branches located in 30 markets by: Envisioning and preparing the Bank’s futures information systems Partnering and supporting core banking flagships and transverse areas in their large-scale development projects Providing premium In-house Banking applications This unique positioning empowers us to bring our core banking business a sustainable competitive advantage on the market. We seek innovative and agile people sharing our mindset to support ambitious and forthcoming technological challenges. Position In a challenging and multicultural environment, we seek a motivated and skilled IT business analyst to join our GIT/ISAP/DEC – Digital Excellence Center . Mission : on a portfolio of transversal projects for various business lines, design, develop and document solutions. Guaranty the alignment between users' needs and products delivered (in-house solutions). You will intervene during the project life cycle within a fully integrated team in Singapore. In the role of IT Business Analyst, you will interface with key stakeholders and other IT teams to apply your proficiency across different stages of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). You will be responsible for gathering and analyzing requirements, building work breakdown structure, providing estimations with help from the technical teams(s), quality control post-development, and steering UAT with end-users. The role is heavily focused business process analysis and on data analysis / design with multiple data sources. A successful candidate will drive innovative ideas across the team, reframe thinking, build consensus, and co-create values by delivering passion for innovation. You will be part of a learning culture where teamwork and collaboration are encouraged, excellence is rewarded, and diversity is respected and valued. Qualifications and Profile This position requires relevant experience as an IT Business Analyst supporting the development of enterprise-wide complex information systems within major organizations, ideally in a banking environment. The level of detail of the knowledge will be assessed considering the seniority of the candidate. Main responsibilities Main responsibilities of the role: Liaise with project stakeholders (onshore IT teams and business users) in defining detailed functional specifications and clarifications. Proactively communicate and collaborate with stakeholders to analyze information needs and functional requirements and deliver the following artefacts as needed: detailed functional specifications, use cases, test strategy and test cases. Analyze and transform business requirements into solutions with the help of Development teams. Prepare, maintain and communicate functional documentation. Plan, prepare and execute the qualification strategy and tests of the target solutions. Perform System Integration Testing (SIT) before allowing transition to the User Acceptance Testing (UAT) phase. Identify test data required to complete the test activities. Steering of the User Acceptance Tests (UAT) and reporting status to project stakeholders. Report and escalate key risks and issues related to project execution to the project manager. Deliver thoroughly tested products on time, up to production deployment. Support the project planning, schedules and milestones in coordination with project teams. Report status to project stakeholders Report and escalate key risks, and issues Prepare and maintain the application user guide and operating guide Provide L2/L3 support on production incidents Work with large data volumes, analysis and troubleshoot of data anomalies/inconsistencies. Legal and Regulatory Responsibilities Comply with all applicable legal, regulatory and internal Compliance requirements, including, but not limited to, the Singapore Compliance manual and Compliance policies and procedures as issued from time to time; Financial Security requirements, including, but not limited to, the prevention of Financial Crime and Fraud including reporting obligations to the Money Laundering Reporting Officer. Maintain appropriate knowledge to ensure to be fully qualified to undertake the role. Complete all mandatory training as required to attain and maintain competence Mandatory Hands-on experience of coordination between the business and IT teams (various locations) and as primary functional support for development teams. Strong ability to interpret business needs and translate them into requirements. Knowledge in software development life cycle and software testing life cycle. Experience in writing test documents and performing functional tests. Risk awareness, ability to evaluate and predict risks and drawback of target solution. Identify and determine enhancements to improve business processes and to mitigate/reduce risks. Proficiency in SQL database queries (complex queries). Ability to prepare dataset for analysis and validation purposes. Strong experience in working with Microsoft Office tools, JIRA. Nice to Have Knowledge of performance testing and associated tools UI/UX experience is a great plus. Banking sector domain knowledge: payments, accounting, SWIFT, global markets, accounting, etc. Other Professional Skills and Mind-set Strong analytical and problem solving skills. Proactive style of working. Strong communication (written, and verbal) and organizational skills. Ability to multi-task and work independently with minimal supervision. Team player mindset. Ability to work under pressure. Understanding and respect of cultural diversity. Note - This is a contract- 12 months with bank. Renewable subject to performance. We offer a competitive remuneration package, consistent with your qualifications and experience. For fair employment practices, we are keen on Singaporeans/SPR ONLY. What The Role Is The Smart Nation Group (SNG), under the Ministry of Digital Development and Information drives key Smart Nation and digital government projects, uplifts the standards of Government ICT and services, builds long-term capabilities for the public sector, and promotes adoption and participation in digital and smart innovations. We are pulling together a whole-of-nation effort, and we will actively engage the public, the industry and fellow agencies to build a Smart Nation together. The Government Data Division (GDD) within SNDG plays a central policymaking role in advancing the management and use of Government data as a national strategic asset. It is responsible for (a) developing and implementing whole-of-Government policies that guide data management, sharing and use across the public sector; (b) identifying and driving new initiatives to improve the exchange of data between Government agencies, and sharing of Government data with commercial partners and the public; and (c) strengthening data governance by developing policies on the responsible use of data, as well as (d) driving the development of capabilities, institutionalising structures and standardising tools. What You Will Be Working On We are looking for someone with experience in policy and ecosystem development. You will be part of a team refreshing strategies, spearheading policies to develop the ecosystemand driving the implementation of high-impact initiatives to advance public sector data governance outcomes. Key initiatives include: Refresh WOG data governance structures and accountability frameworks, to ensure role clarity, and governance levers across the ecosystem Design and strengthen system-level incentives and enablers to catalyse good data governance outcomes, e.g. articulating what “good” looks like, what baseline requirements are, design performance measurement tool This role offers a unique opportunity work with stakeholders to shape policies at a systems level and drive their implementation across the public sector. What We Are Looking For We are looking for dynamic and self-motivated individuals who value integrity, teamwork and excellence. If you share our passion in shaping Singapore into a Smart Nation, we welcome you to join us! Candidates should have: Background in related domains such as public policy, Business, Law, Information/Computer Science, Data Analytics/Statistics, Engineering, or any related field Relevant experience is a bonus: Policy and strategy, strategic planning Data Governance and policy Ecosystem development Regulatory compliance Data management Data privacy regulations The ideal candidate possesses the following skills: Experience in policy and ecosystem development is a bonus Strong systems perspective and able to see the big picture Good perspective taking skills, able to analyse issues from different agencies’ standpoints Strong communication and interpersonal skills that can create a consensus on action from a wide spectrum of external and internal stakeholders Work with cross-functional teams, including IT, legal and business stakeholders Resourceful and proactive, particularly in proposing alternate solutions to achieve desired outcomes Analytical and creative Excellent written communication skills Note: The traineeship will be for a period of 6 months. Save Report this job Google will be prioritizing applicants who have a current right to work in Singapore, and do not require Google's sponsorship of a visa. Minimum qualifications: Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. 4 years of experience in advertising business, consulting, media, or with advertising agencies, or experience gathering and presenting technical data and insights to promote products and solutions. 4 years of experience working in a customer-facing role. 3 years of experience using the sales lifecycle, consulting, or strategy to drive business. Ability to communicate in English and either French, German, or other EU languages fluently to interact with local clients and stakeholders. Preferred qualifications: Experience in a sales or client-engagement capacity. Experience developing grounded, insight-driven narratives in mobile, digital, and offline mediums into actionable business opportunities. Experience working in Europe or knowledge of Europe markets/regions. Knowledge of digital advertising. Ability to tell stories with data and excellent communication, analytical, project management, teamwork, presentation, and organizational skills. About the job Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. You bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. You build trusted relationships with customers, uncovering their business needs and translating them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. Google's Large Customer Sales (LCS) teams are strategic partners and industry thought leaders to the world's leading brands and agencies. We continuously challenge how customers think about their business and how Google can support growth. We focus on helping these players navigate profound industry shifts and drive outsized business performance by competitively selling Google's full suite of advertising solutions across Search, YouTube, Measurement, and more. As a member of our LCS team, you'll have the unique opportunity to sell at the forefront of technology, collaborating with executives, influencing market-shaping strategies, and delivering tangible results that significantly impact major global businesses and drive the growth of Google. Responsibilities Identify high-impact international growth opportunities based on existing frameworks and tools, and develop new ideas and approaches to drive international growth and scale for an assigned portfolio. Understand customers' objectives and be a digital consultant to businesses in APAC looking for international opportunities. Understand the market landscape and develop and execute projects around international growth, impacting clients' understanding of global markets. Contribute to growing international growth acumen and resources within Google and support other members in your network by sharing knowledge about your assigned market. Build thorough knowledge related to advertising on Google, Google Display Network, and YouTube. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .
