Minimum qualifications: Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. 4 years of experience gathering and presenting technical and financial data to promote products, services, and solutions. 4 years of experience in business development, partnerships, management consulting, advertising business, media, or working with advertising agencies. Ability to communicate in English and Vietnamese fluently to communicate with and support Vietnamese speaking customers. Preferred qualifications: MBA or advanced professional degree in a relevant field. Experience consulting with app developers, including familiarity with gaming app monetization and marketing strategies. Familiarity with the cultural and business nuances of various countries, particularly in Asia. Ability to turn data-driven narratives into actionable revenue opportunities, specifically within mobile and digital mediums. Ability to communicate effectively and align multiple internal and external stakeholders toward shared goals. About The Job Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. You bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. You build trusted relationships with customers, uncovering their business needs and translating them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. As an expert on international growth success factors and member of a global network that you can leverage, you are a trusted advisor and partner to our App Developers. From strategic planning, identifying the right target markets, to setting them up for success within their non-domestic footprint, you provide insights and support and help them reach and exceed their international ambitions. Working closely with our advertisers, you develop a deep understanding of their business needs, objectives and decision processes. Your entrepreneurial spirit, strategic selling, communication and problem solving abilities allow you to identify the biggest levers for success, and present unique insights that shape our customers’ plans. Based on your knowledge of online media and in-depth understanding of our product suite, you create Google-caliber solutions that turn these plans into results. The Global Business Organization (GBO) is the engine that powers Google’s full ecosystem of products and services to help customers and partners succeed and grow. GBO includes the commercial arms of Ads sellers, business development teams, and customer services and support, as well as overlay programs that enable coordinated engagement with Google's most complex and important customers and partners. Responsibilities Develop high-touch relationships to serve as a trusted consultant with major international customers to fuel their advertising growth. Understand Apps customers’ objectives, and help them achieve these by developing forward-thinking, data-driven export sales recommendations and by educating them on wider export-related topics and opportunities. Identify high-impact export opportunities based on existing frameworks and tools, and develop new ideas and approaches to drive export growth for the assigned portfolio. Partner with sales and cross-functional teams to manage end-to-end support from expansion strategy to campaign execution. Participate and lead external workshops and trainings to educate customers beyond your assigned portfolio on export opportunities and strategies. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. Ryeonbeen K. Talent Acquisition Manager, Korea & Japan+ at Digital Edge Who we are: Digital Edge DC (Digital Edge) is a leading data center platform company, established to transform digital infrastructure in Asia. We seek to build the foundation for the world’s digital future, helping organizations to grow sustainably and empowering the populations they serve. Through building and operating state-of-the-art, energy-efficient data centers rich with connectivity options, we bring new colocation and interconnect options to the Asian market, making infrastructure deployment in the region easy, efficient and economical. Backed by Stonepeak, a leading alternative investment firm specializing in infrastructure and real assets, Digital Edge has in excess of US$1.6 billion in committed capital. Founded in late 2019, the company has grown rapidly across multiple markets in the region, with 30+ data centers in operations and under construction, and this role is an exciting opportunity to join the team as we further expand our footprint across Asia. Our values: Respect: We embrace diversity and collaboration. Innovation: We share ideas and solve problems. Strive: We are driven and determined. Excellence: We seek to deliver the best. Responsibility: We do what’s right for our people and the planet. What we need: Reporting to the Sr. Manager, Enterprise Applications, and this role will be responsible being the critical bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams, driving the design, implementation, and optimization of Salesforce solutions across CPQ, Service Cloud, and Revenue Cloud. You will play a key role in shaping scalable business processes and delivering measurable outcomes. This role will be based in Hong Kong. Key responsibilities: Elicit, analyze, and document complex business requirements from stakeholders across Sales, Finance, and Customer Success teams Lead end-to-end requirements gathering, process mapping, and solution design for Salesforce CPQ, Service Cloud, and Revenue Cloud implementations Translate business needs into detailed functional specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria for development teams Configure and support Salesforce CPQ features including product catalog, pricing rules, discount schedules, quote templates, and approval workflows Support Revenue Cloud initiatives including billing, invoicing, revenue recognition, and subscription management Design and optimize Service Cloud processes including case management, entitlements, SLAs, knowledge base, and omni-channel routing Collaborate with Solution Architects and Developers to ensure technical solutions align with business objectives Conduct gap analysis between current-state processes and desired future-state Salesforce capabilities Facilitate workshops, sprint planning sessions, and UAT (User Acceptance Testing) with business users Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation including process flows, data dictionaries, and BRDs Act as a subject matter expert (SME) and trusted advisor to business stakeholders on Salesforce best practices Monitor post-implementation performance and proactively identify areas for improvement Support data migration activities and ensure data integrity across platforms Mentor junior business analysts and contribute to team capability building The successful candidate: 3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst with a strong focus on Salesforce platforms Hands-on experience with Salesforce CPQ (product configuration, pricing, quoting, approval processes) Proven experience with Salesforce Revenue Cloud (billing, subscriptions, revenue lifecycle management) Solid working knowledge of Salesforce Service Cloud (case management, entitlements, knowledge, omni-channel) Strong understanding of the Quote-to-Cash (Q2C) and Order-to-Revenue (O2R) business processes Experience writing user stories, process flows, and functional specs in Agile/Scrum environments Proficiency with tools such as Jira, Confluence, Lucidchart, or similar Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders simultaneously Salesforce certifications preferred: CPQ Specialist , Service Cloud Consultant , Business Analyst , or Administrator Join us to shape the future of Digital Infrastructure in Asia. Apply now for a confidential career discussion. Base pay range $50.00/hr - $95.00/hr Financial Analyst (AI Training) About The Role What if your financial expertise could directly shape how AI understands the world of business, markets, and corporate performance? We're looking for Financial Analysts to review financial statements, validate key metrics, and deliver concise, structured evaluations that help train the next generation of AI systems. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role. No AI background needed — just a sharp analytical mind and solid experience in financial analysis or corporate finance. Organization: Alignerr Type: Hourly Contract Location: Remote Commitment: Flexible hours What You'll Do Analyze company-level financial statements and operational data Validate key financial metrics, ratios, and performance trends Cross-reference narrative disclosures with underlying quantitative data Summarize findings in clear, concise written evaluations Maintain consistency and accuracy across recurring evaluation cycles Support structured dataset validation for AI training purposes Who You Are Experienced in financial analysis, accounting, or corporate finance Proficient with spreadsheets and comfortable working with large datasets Detail-oriented — you spot inconsistencies others miss A clear, concise communicator who can translate complex data into plain language Self-motivated and reliable when working independently on a flexible schedule No prior AI or tech experience required Nice to Have Understanding of sector-specific financial drivers and industry benchmarks Experience with equity research, credit analysis, or financial modeling Familiarity with reading earnings reports, 10-Ks, or regulatory filings Background in investment banking, consulting, or corporate FP&A Why Join Us Work on cutting-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, task-based work Apply your financial expertise to a domain that's shaping the future of technology Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch Show more Show less Seniority level Entry level Employment type Contract Job function Finance and Sales Industries Technology, Information and Internet Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Alignerr by 2x See who you know Get notified about new Financial Analyst jobs in Singapore . Save Report this job Position Overview The Next Generation Electronic Medical Record (NGEMR) is one of the three major healthcare programmes that Singapore has embarked on to transform care delivery in Singapore. It is an advanced centralised electronic medical record system that provides a single patient record across institutions of the National Healthcare Group (NHG) and the National University Health System (NUHS) to enhance care management and delivery. Through NGEMR, the entire patient journey across different care settings is recorded, enabling patient-centric care and seamless care transition. NGEMR also supports clinical decision with its intelligent alerts and data analytical capabilities. The aggregation of data translates to insights for medical education, research and population management to achieve better health outcomes. Role & Responsibilities Meeting with report requesters (SMEs) to understand their data needs. Identifying and reviewing the appropriate technological solutions for various reporting needs. Creating reports, using a third-party report writing tool such as Crystal reports, to display Clarity data. Writing SQL for custom SQL metrics. Modifying existing reports, as necessary. Distributing Clarity reports to report consumers using Epic-Crystal integration. Reviewing peer reports for validity. Testing report build in a specified testing environment and volume testing in live environments. Reviewing upgrade documentation related to Clarity features and released Crystal reporting content and determining how to implement the changes. BIDs might also be responsible for building: Dashboards Batch Extracts to downstream systems Epic reporting tools (SlicerDicer, RWB, Cogito SQL etc.) Third-party tools (QlikView/Tableau etc.) Requirements A degree in engineering, physics, computer sciences, math, information systems, or statistics. Proficiency in SQL with hands-on experience and relational database concepts. Strong communication skills Critical thinking and an analytical mind. Technically proficient in your analytical database platforms. Working knowledge of the database operating systems. Familiarity with Epic applications and database structure, through training that occurs early in the implementation. Ability to gain an in-depth understanding of the data model through data model classes geared toward content areas. Ability to understand data structures that could benefit the entire reporting environment. Apply Now NOTE: It only takes a few minutes to apply for a meaningful career in HealthTech - GO FOR IT!! Direct message the job poster from Optimum Solutions Pte Ltd Siddharth Gaind Siddharth Gaind Senior Vice President at Optimum Solutions Title: AI Analyst Client : Hospitality Domain Department: IT Infrastructure Operations Team What our client is looking for: Job Duties: Support the AI infrastructure to enable stakeholders to identify business processes that can be enhanced or automated using AI/ML. Support the AI infrastructure to enable stakeholders to conduct feasibility studies to determine the viability of AI solutions (predictive analytics, computer vision, NLP) against business constraints. Work closely with stakeholders to ensure data pipelines lifecycle. Perform root cause analysis on AI infrastructure for any model failures or unexpected outputs. Enable prompt-engineer Large Language Models (GPT, Claude, local Qwen) Enable Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines, ensuring data context is accurate and hallucination rates are low. Perform AI infrastructure benchmarking Act as resources for AI related operation, project implementation, local LLM deployment, capacity planning and troubleshooting. Handle AI related frameworks and python packages upgrade. Deal with configuration and troubleshooting for Nvidia hardware and relevant drivers including CUDA. Integrate AI hardware and inference framework plus application Requirements: Experience Proficiency with Linux environment and python Languages: proficiency in Python (Pandas, Numpy, Scikit-learn), R. SQL : Advanced ability to write complex queries and work with relational databases (Snowflake, BigQuery, PostgreSQL). BI Tools : Experience with visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI). MLOps : Familiarity with model tracking tools (MLflow, Weights & Biases) and cloud platforms (AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, GCP Vertex AI). LLM Stack : Experience with LangChain or vector databases PGVector is desirable. Data science experience such as airflow implementation and relevant data pipeline configuration. Experience with MLOps pipelines and CI/CD for machine learning. Familiarity with Docker and Kubernetes. Implementation and maintenance of local AI inference making use of open source models like Qwen3.5 Education Bachelor’s degree in information technology or equivalent relevant qualifications Preferred with AI related certificates Skills / Competencies Ability to communicate effectively in English and Cantonese/Mandarin Good listening skills & customer service expertise Ability to work as part of a team to accomplish common goals Log, troubleshoot, and track problems to a successful conclusion Ability to present ideas in user-friendly language to non-technical personnel and end users Show more Show less Seniority level Entry level Employment type Contract Job function Information Technology Industries IT Services and IT Consulting, Hospitality, and Data Infrastructure and Analytics Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Optimum Solutions Pte Ltd by 2x See who you know Get notified about new Analyst jobs in Hong Kong, Hong Kong SAR . Direct message the job poster from Ampstek Mohammed Fakrudeen ( James) Mohammed Fakrudeen ( James) Talent Acquisition Lead Hi Role: Data Analyst (Citizen and PR only) Location: Singapore Contract Position Summary We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Group Business Insights team, responsible for transforming raw data into reliable, well-structured, and insightful datasets that support business analysis and decision-making. This role focuses on data preparation, analysis, validation, and modeling, with close collaboration with internal stakeholders. The ideal candidate brings strong SQL and Python expertise, a solid understanding of data structures and analytics workflows, and a mindset oriented toward data quality, problem-solving, and business understanding. Key Responsibilities Data Analysis & Preparation • Perform data cleaning, transformation, and preparation from multiple structured data sources • Conduct data exploration and analysis to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies • Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reliability through validation checks and reconciliation • Apply strong data modeling principles to support downstream analytics and insights Analytics & Technical Execution • Write efficient, scalable, and well-documented SQL queries for data extraction and analysis • Use Python for data manipulation, analysis, and automation of analytical workflows • Work with large datasets using platforms and tools such as BigQuery, PySpark, and Airflow • Follow good coding practices, version control standards, and documentation using Git Stakeholder Collaboration • Engage with internal teams and business users to understand data requirements and analytical needs • Translate business questions into analytical problem statements and structured data solutions • Support stakeholders with data understanding, interpretation, and clarification Governance & Quality • Contribute to data governance practices, ensuring compliance with data quality and usage standards • Proactively identify data issues and recommend improvements to data pipelines and structures Required Skills & Qualifications - Technical (Mandatory) • Strong proficiency in SQL for data querying and analysis • Hands-on experience with Python for data analysis • Solid understanding of data modeling concepts • Experience working with BigQuery, Airflow, PySpark Good to Have • Experience with Excel / Google Sheets for analysis and validation • Exposure to BI tools such as Power Bl, Tableau, or Looker • Working knowledge of statistics and analytical techniques • Familiarity with advanced analytics (regression, forecasting, experimentation) Experience & Education • 3-6 years of relevant experience in data analysis or analytics roles • Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience) Soft Skills & Competencies • Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking • Clear and effective communication skills • Ability to perform requirement gathering and ask the right analytical questions • Storytelling with data to support understanding and alignment • Consulting mindset with attention to detail, ownership, and accountability Stakeholder Interaction • Regular interaction with internal analytics teams and business users • Minimal extern^ Regards James Lead APAC Recruiter | Amsptek LLC Email: James@ampstek.com Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Contract Job function Information Technology Industries IT Services and IT Consulting Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Ampstek by 2x See who you know Get notified when a new job is posted. Save Report this job Team Overview Our Hong Kong team of 5 sits within the wider US/UK tax team, based in the UK, of over 70 people, helping US and UK citizens and businesses based away from home to manage their net earnings and reduce liabilities. Whilst our Hong Kong team looks after US nationals living across Southeast Asia, the wider team consists of dual-qualified US/UK tax experts, 14 partners and strong administration support. Our team know both tax systems inside out and have handled relocations for solo workers, and up to hundreds of employees. Role Overview This opportunity sits within our Hong Kong team and will require the successful individual to handle a portfolio of clients, delivering a quality compliance and advisory service to both clients and Managers, as well as identifying opportunities to cross sell the firm’s services. In order to become fully qualified in US taxation, you will be provided with study support towards the Enrolled Agent qualification. What You’ll Be Doing Act principally as a preparer of US tax returns for a portfolio of clients within the Expatriate Tax team but at the same time, look for and notify the client of opportunities in which taxes can be minimised. Manage a portfolio of up to 150-180 clients, consulting senior team members, where applicable Deal with ad hoc advice requested by clients, consulting with Managers and Directors where necessary (to ensure that the firm’s review processes are satisfied). Liaise with your manager in connection with monitoring the management of your portfolio to include: accountability for chargeable and non-chargeable hours and projected billing. Recognise opportunities for cross-selling of services and “adding value”. Keep up to date with technical developments of tax law in the US. Assist in the administration of the Expatriate Tax team as and when requested and when time allows. Take responsibility in developing one’s IT skills and abilities so that IT equipment may be used to its fullest extent and seeking help and training as appropriate. What’s In It For You We go above and beyond for our clients, but also for you. We have a genuine commitment to your development, offering you a closely supported and structured programme of tuition, provided by a top firm of external tutors. Internally, you’ll be supported by a manager who will act as your mentor, partners, senior staff and a dedicated Learning & Development team. As you progress through your training you will receive incremental salary increases as you hit various milestones in your qualification. What We're Looking For 2.1 degree (or equivalent) HKDSE (or equivalent): Level 4, 4, 4 excluding Liberal Studies and Citizenship and Social Development. HKDSE (or equivalent): Level 4 in English Language and Maths MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) Please note we do not accept General Studies or applications from part-qualified applicants. What You’ll Bring Excellent interpersonal skills in order to interact professionally with internal and external clients. Able to prioritise workload and be self-sufficient; multi-tasker; problem-solving skills. Displays ethical judgement. Why Buzzacott? We’re big enough to provide deep expertise across a wide range of specialisms, but small enough to value meaningful personal connections — with clients and colleagues alike. You’ll work alongside experienced professionals who will support your continued development, while you contribute to shaping the growth of both our team and our clients. At Buzzacott, we’re committed to equal opportunities and ensuring all employment decisions are fair, objective, and based on merit. If you are interested in this role, we encourage you to apply directly via the Apply button at the top of the page. When applying, please include your salary expectations and let us know what interests you about the role. Alternatively, if you would like to talk to one of our HR Team about the vacancy before applying, contact us at traineerecruitment@buzzacott.co.uk Please note: Our HR Team review and respond to all applications No agencies please. Direct message the job poster from True Home Harsh Pinjani Harsh Pinjani E-Commerce Manager | SEM | PPC | SEO | User Acquisition | Retention | Product Ranking | Revenue Growth Role Description The Consulting Analyst / Research Consultant plays a key role in supporting strategic decision-making through rigorous research, structured analysis, and insight generation. This role focuses on investigating complex business questions, synthesizing large volumes of information, and translating findings into clear, actionable recommendations. Working across a variety of topics, the position contributes to both advisory engagements and internal strategic initiatives. Core responsibilities include conducting qualitative and quantitative research, analyzing industry trends, and developing data-driven insights to support client or organizational objectives. The role involves building analytical models, preparing reports and presentations, and distilling complex information into compelling narratives for stakeholders. Collaboration is essential, as the position works closely with cross-functional teams to frame problems, validate hypotheses, and deliver high-quality outputs. The Consulting Analyst / Research Consultant is expected to demonstrate strong intellectual curiosity, attention to detail, and the ability to think critically under ambiguity. Success in this role requires balancing analytical rigor with practical thinking, ensuring that recommendations are both insightful and implementable. The position also demands clear communication skills and the ability to manage multiple workstreams efficiently. Qualifications Strong research and analytical skills, with the ability to gather, evaluate, and synthesize information from diverse sources Proficiency in both qualitative and quantitative analysis, including data interpretation and structured problem-solving Ability to develop clear, logical frameworks and translate findings into actionable recommendations Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with strong presentation capabilities High level of attention to detail and commitment to accuracy Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines in a fast-paced environment Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work effectively across teams Intellectual curiosity and a proactive approach to learning and problem-solving Professionalism, adaptability, and sound judgment in handling complex business topics Show more Show less Seniority level Entry level Employment type Part-time Job function Business Development and Sales Industries Real Estate Agents and Brokers Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at True Home by 2x See who you know Get notified when a new job is posted. Our client is a leading global financial services firm renowned for providing a wide range of investment banking, securities, and wealth management services. They are currently seeking a Business Analyst specializing in Client Services to serve as a strategic partner to the Product Owner of their Client Reporting platform. This is a pivotal role focused on delivering a scalable, world-class experience through sophisticated reporting solutions that cover regulatory, accounting, and complex data considerations. This role is an exceptional opportunity for an individual looking to deepen their expertise within financial services. By supporting a strategic system used across the region, you will gain deep exposure to sophisticated investment products and global reporting principles. It is a great environment to build upon your functional analysis skills and move toward a more product-focused mindset, working alongside experienced stakeholders to help shape the regional roadmap. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Alignment: Support the Product Owner in defining and refining the product vision, long-term roadmap, and strategic priorities for the regional reporting system. Cross-Functional Synergy: Partner closely with Client Servicing, Finance, Operations, and Technology stakeholders to synthesize complex business requirements. Agile Delivery: Translate high-level client needs into precise functional requirements, user stories, and rigorous acceptance criteria. Quality Assurance: Lead end-to-end project delivery, including the coordination of User Acceptance Testing (UAT), issue tracking, and ensuring timely resolution for seamless implementations. Implementation & Readiness: Facilitate release planning and business readiness activities to ensure smooth post-implementation support and stakeholder communication. Qualifications & Experience: 3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, with a strong preference for candidates from Financial Services/Investment Banking background. Hands-on experience with data-driven platforms, portfolio reporting, or client reporting systems. A solid grasp of investment products, accounting principles, and the regulatory landscape within financial services. Exceptional stakeholder management skills with proficiency in English ; fluency in Chinese (Cantonese or Putonghua) is considered highly advantageous for this position. Familiarity with Agile / SDLC delivery methodologies is beneficial. A proactive and structured approach to problem-solving in a dynamic, high-stakes environment. A deep commitment to enhancing the client experience through reporting accuracy and operational efficiency. To Apply: Please apply directly to this posting or email us your Resume/CV to recruiting@pfccgroup.com. Due to the high volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. About PFCC Group: We partner with the world’s most sophisticated financial institutions to unlock sustainable value. By merging operational excellence with the power of AI and digital transformation, we ensure our clients are equipped to lead, not just keep pace. Bridging the Gap between Vision and Technical Mastery, we believe that even the most advanced strategy is only as powerful as the people executing it. That is why we specialize in identifying and placing high-calibre technical talent within the world’s premier financial ecosystems. Whether it’s securing mission-critical infrastructure or driving global digital shifts, we connect elite IT professionals with opportunities at global financial leaders. Direct message the job poster from Fano (Fano Labs) Skylar YANG Skylar YANG Human Resources｜Tech For Good｜We Are Hiring｜Look for talents who specialise in AI/AI Language business Overview: We are looking for a Product Business Analyst who combines strong business analysis skills with hands-on quality assurance capabilities. In this hybrid role, you will act as a key liaison between business stakeholders and technical teams, ensuring that product features meet real-world business needs and are delivered with high quality. You will be involved throughout the product lifecycle—from gathering requirements and designing workflows to executing test plans and supporting client success. ** We are hiring for two distinct focuses within this role: one for internal projects and one for external client-facing deployments. Key Responsibilities: 1. Business Analysis & Documentation Gather and analyze user requirements and translate them into clear, actionable specifications. Prepare documentation such as user requirement documents (URD), use cases, user manuals, and UAT test plans. Collaborate with Product Managers and Customer Success teams to align product features with business goals. 2.Quality Assurance & Testing Design and execute comprehensive test plans, including functional, regression, and end-to-end testing. Develop test cases based on real customer workflows and data patterns. Identify, document, and track defects; perform root cause analysis and ensure timely resolution. Support multilingual testing and incorporate client-reported issues into test coverage. 3.Client Support & Issue Management Act as an application specialist to support client onboarding and training. Investigate and respond to client-reported issues, including log analysis and issue tracking. Provide professional support and ensure a smooth user experience across enterprise products. 4.Cross-Functional Collaboration & Process Improvement Work closely with internal teams (Engineering, Product, QA, Support) and external partners to ensure project success. Participate in Agile ceremonies to align testing and development priorities. Continuously improve testing processes and contribute to automation and documentation standards. Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related fields. 1-2 years of experience in business analysis, QA, or software implementation roles. Solid understanding of SaaS products, cloud/on-premise environments, and web/app technologies. Hands-on experience with SQL, API testing, and defect tracking tools (e.g., JIRA). Familiarity with test automation tools (e.g., Selenium, Cypress) is a plus. Strong communication and presentation skills; fluent in English (Cantonese and Mandarin are a plus). Fast learner with a proactive mindset and strong analytical thinking. Direct message the job poster from Presto YouJin Park YouJin Park About Presto Founded in 2014, Presto is an algorithmic trading and financial services firm with a global presence. Presto focuses on delivering exceptional value for clients through a rigorous research-driven approach to investment and trade execution. With more than a 100 million trade executions in a day, Presto is a leading financial services firm in both digital assets and traditional finance markets. About Middle Office The Middle Office is a core pillar of our business, handling risk management, performance evaluation, compliance, business development and trading operations. While our foundation is quantitative and automated, we are now expanding our capabilities to proactively drive business growth and optimize our global trading footprint. About Role We are seeking a proactive junior professional to join our Middle Office team to support counterparty onboarding with banks, brokers, exchanges, and other financial service providers across the diverse markets in which Presto Group operates. In this role, the candidate will primarily focus on the end-to-end corporate onboarding and periodic KYB process for Presto Group of entities, while supporting the Senior Business Development Manager in market expansion initiatives. This position is ideal for a junior candidate who has a genuine interest in financial markets, is highly detail-oriented, and is eager to build knowledge in buy-side trading operations. Responsibilities New onboarding and periodic KYB: Support new onboarding and periodic KYB processes by tracking requirements, coordinating document collection, preparing submission packs, and following up on outstanding items to ensure timely completion Counterparty Onboarding Coordination: Liaise with banks, brokers, exchanges, and other external counterparties on onboarding requirements, account opening documentation, KYB queries, account setup, and account maintenance matters Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with internal teams to gather required information, monitor action items, and follow up with stakeholders until completion Required 1 to 2 years of experience in onboarding, KYB, KYC, legal/compliance support or corporate administration, gained from financial institutions (e.g. banks, brokers, proprietary trading firms) or professional firms (e.g. CPAs, law firms, corporate service providers) Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple requests, deadlines, and follow-up in a structured and timely manner Meticulous, detail-oriented, with a high standard of accuracy in documentation preparation Proactive, reliable, and disciplined in meeting deadlines under time-sensitive conditions while maintaining work quality Clear written and verbal communication skills to coordinate effectively with internal and external stakeholders professionally Growth mindset, willingness to learn, take ownership of assigned tasks, and adapt to changing onboarding requirements across different markets and counterparties Preferred Hands-on experience with large language model (LLM) platforms is a plus Comfortable working with data, spreadsheets, trackers, and presentation materials Note The job description provided is a general outline of the role and responsibilities. It is not intended to be exhaustive and may be subject to change based on the needs of the company. Position Financial Analyst - FP&A Job Description Business Title: Financial Analyst Reporting to the Senior Manager, Finance, APAC FP&A based in Hong Kong, you will gain exposure working with different stakeholders and regional projects to contribute to our business growth. What You’ll Be Doing Support financial analysis for Regional and Business Unit performance from Sales top-lines to other KPI analysis. Assist in variance analysis against forecast, budget, and expenditure analysis by Business Units & Functions. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather financial data. Assist in the preparation of management business review package and analysis for monitoring KPIs and financial metrics. Participate in and coordinate with the monthly forecasting and annual budgeting for the APAC Components business. Partner with the business leaders for cost evaluation and financial modelling Conduct ad-hoc financial analysis as needed. What We Are Looking For Degree in Finance, Accounting and/or related fields At least 5 years of financial reporting and analysis experience, Oracle experience is preferable CPA is preferable Strong communication and stakeholder management skills Proficient in PC skills and expert in MS Excel Knowledge in Hyperion Planning and PBI will be an advantage Good command of English, Cantonese, and Mandarin Ability to adapt quickly, work independently and be result-oriented Proactive, a good team player, and self-motivated What’s In It For You At Arrow, we recognize that financial rewards and great benefits are important aspects of an ideal job. That’s why we offer competitive financial compensation, including various compensation plans, and a solid benefits package. Medical, Dental Insurance Life Insurance Discretionary Double Pay Performance Bonus Paid Time Off Special/Extra Leave 5-Day Work Week Free Shuttle Bus Growth Opportunities And more! About Arrow Arrow Electronics, Inc. (NYSE: ARW), an award-winning Fortune 133 and one of Fortune Magazine’s Most Admired Companies. Arrow guides innovation forward for over 220,000 leading technology manufacturers and service providers. With 2023 sales of USD $33.11 billion, Arrow develops technology solutions that improve business and daily life. Our broad portfolio that spans the entire technology landscape helps customers create, make and manage forward-thinking products that make the benefits of technology accessible to as many people as possible. Learn more at www.arrow.com. Our strategic direction of guiding innovation forward is expressed as Five Years Out, a way of thinking about the tangible future to bridge the gap between what's possible and the practical technologies to make it happen. Direct message the job poster from Value FM Ashim Basak Ashim Basak Student At Christ University Bengaluru Role Description The Strategy Analyst / Asset Management role supports the development, execution, and monitoring of investment and portfolio strategies across asset classes. This position focuses on delivering data-driven insights that enhance portfolio performance, optimize asset allocation, and support long-term value creation. The role works closely with investment professionals and cross-functional teams to evaluate market trends, assess risk-return dynamics, and inform strategic investment decisions. Key responsibilities include conducting in-depth financial and market analysis, supporting portfolio construction and rebalancing decisions, and evaluating performance against benchmarks. The role also involves building and maintaining analytical models to assess asset performance, scenario outcomes, and macroeconomic impacts. Additionally, the Strategy Analyst contributes to investment research, due diligence processes, and the identification of new opportunities across public and private markets. A strong emphasis is placed on synthesizing complex financial data into clear, actionable insights. The role requires the ability to interpret macroeconomic developments, industry trends, and company-level fundamentals to support investment strategy formulation. Effective communication is essential, as findings and recommendations are regularly presented to senior stakeholders and investment committees. Qualifications Strong analytical and quantitative skills with the ability to interpret financial and market data Solid understanding of investment principles, portfolio theory, and asset management concepts Proficiency in financial modeling, valuation techniques, and scenario analysis Ability to conduct research and synthesize insights from multiple data sources Strong critical thinking and problem-solving abilities with a structured approach to analysis Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex ideas clearly High attention to detail and strong organizational skills in managing analytical work Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment Strong business acumen and interest in financial markets and macroeconomic trends Collaborative mindset with the ability to work across investment and strategy teams Show more Show less Seniority level Entry level Employment type Part-time Job function Business Development and Sales Industries Facilities Services Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Value FM by 2x See who you know Get notified when a new job is posted. HR at Groupe SEB Regional Assistant Data Analyst We are seeking a Regional Assistant Data Analyst to support data-driven initiatives across the APAC region. This role focuses on web data mining, data analysis, AI, and dashboard development to enhance business intelligence and market efficiency. This position sits within the Regional Sales & Marketing team and supports data initiatives across APAC. What’s in it for you: 1 . Data Mining & Delivery Support the setup, maintenance, and enhancement of regional web data mining across multiple e-commerce platforms. Build and maintain dashboards in Qlik Sense to deliver actionable reporting and analysis. Translate raw data into actionable insights and visual reports for business stakeholders. 2 . Data Analysis & Reporting Conduct online and offline data analysis to support overall business performance and decision-making. Perform advance analysis on online cross-channel metrics (e.g. media, investment) to generate actionable insights. Provide analytical support for regional initiatives and cross-functional teams. Prepare regular reports and presentations to communicate key findings, trends, and recommendations to stakeholders. Leverage AWS, Snowflake, Qlik Sense, Python, and Power Platform to develop automated reports and dashboards. 3 . AI Initiatives Support the development and testing of AI-driven solutions to improve data processing, insights generation, and automation. Support the use of AI to generate insights, automate reporting, and turn complex data into clear business recommendations for regional and local stakeholders. 4 . Market Efficiency Support the development of dashboards and analytical tools to improve market efficiency. What we are looking for: Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Analytics, or a related field. 0–2 years of relevant experience (internships or project experience acceptable). Basic knowledge of Python, SQL, or other data processing tools. Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g. Qlik Sense, Power BI, Tableau) is a plus. Interest in web scraping, automation, and AI applications. Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills. Good communication skills in English and Cantonese. We offer attractive remuneration package to the right candidate including 5-day work, double pay, discretionary bonus, family leave & birthday leave, medical, dental / life insurance scheme, etc. Interested parties please send your full resume with detailed working experience, availability, current and expected salary to Human Resources Department by clicking the apply button. Company Overview Groupe SEB, the world leader in small household equipment, with operations in almost 150 countries, over 8 Billion euros sales and earned a strong position on all continents through the diversified product ranges and multi-brand strategy consisting of world famous brands (Tefal, Lagostina, WMF, Krups, Rowenta, Moulinex…..)and local brands. Multi-cultural and multi-creative, Groupe SEB has 34,000 employees in 60 countries, share the same values and commitment to sustainable development, the same sense of professionalism and passion for innovation. SEB Asia, our Hong Kong Office comprises a regional team for Asia Pacific, strategic marketing for global categories, commercial team for Hong Kong and Southeast Asia markets and also our Group Asia Sourcing platform to develop new products offers for our global markets. To fuel its sustainable growth and long-term strategy, we are inviting talented and energetic individual to join our professional team. Save Report this job Do you want your voice heard and your actions to count? Discover your opportunity with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s leading financial groups. Across the globe, we’re 150,000 colleagues, striving to make a difference for every client, organization, and community we serve. We stand for our values, building long-term relationships, serving society, and fostering shared and sustainable growth for a better world. With a vision to be the world’s most trusted financial group, it’s part of our culture to put people first, listen to new and diverse ideas and collaborate toward greater innovation, speed and agility. This means investing in talent, technologies, and tools that empower you to own your career. Join MUFG, where being inspired is expected and making a meaningful impact is rewarded. Job Summary: The role is part of the Planning, Governance and Regulatory Affairs Department in Compliance Office for Asia, supporting the development and implementation of an enhanced regulatory change management framework to mitigate MUFG Bank’s risk from new/changing legal and regulatory requirements at a regional or Singapore level. This role plays a key part in ensuring MUFG Bank continues to enhance its controls and respond to a changing regulatory environment in Singapore and Asia Pacific. The individual reports to the Head of Regional Regulatory Affairs and Change Management, while working directly with Singapore leads for local matters. The successful candidate will have excellent analytical and writing skills, a proactive attitude and be able to work independently to balance priorities & stakeholders. Exposure to or experience in Core Compliance (non-Financial Crime) areas and Singapore regulation is preferred, but the individual will also be supported to develop expertise over time.The Regulatory Change Management (RCM) Analyst supports the end‑to‑end regulatory change management process by assisting with the identification, assessment, tracking, and reporting of regulatory developments applicable to the business. The role focuses on execution, coordination, documentation, and monitoring, under the guidance of senior RCM or Team Heads, to help ensure timely and effective regulatory implementation. Regulatory Monitoring & Identification Monitor assigned regulatory sources (e.g. regulators’ websites, circulars, consultation papers) for new, amended, or upcoming regulatory requirements within the defined scope. Log identified regulatory changes into the approved GRC system or regulatory tracking tool in a timely and accurate manner. Maintain regulatory inventories and support horizon-scanning activities Impact Assessment Support Assist senior RCM or Compliance staff in preparing regulatory applicability and impact assessments, including collation of background information and drafting of initial summaries. Coordinate with relevant stakeholders (Compliance Advisory, Legal, Risk, Operations, Business units) to gather input for impact assessments. Document outcomes, assumptions, and supporting evidence in line with internal standards. Action Tracking & Follow-up Track assigned regulatory changes and related implementation actions, ensuring updates are recorded accurately in the system. Monitor due dates and follow up with action owners on progress and completion status. Escalate overdue items or issues to senior RCM / Compliance staff for further action. Documentation & Record Keeping Maintain complete and orderly records of regulatory changes, assessments, approvals, and supporting documents. Ensure documentation meets internal governance, audit, and regulatory expectations. Support periodic reviews to confirm records remain current and accurate. Reporting & Governance Support Assist in the preparation of management information (MI), dashboards, and summary reports for internal committees and oversight forums. Support regulatory change reporting to local management, regional Compliance (ACO), or GCD as required. Stakeholder Coordination Act as a coordination point for routine regulatory change matters, liaising with internal stakeholders to collect information and updates. Support senior RCM staff in responding to internal queries, audits, or regulatory reviews related to regulatory change management. Job Requirements: Degree qualified with at least two years’ relevant experience in compliance functions within financial institutions or similar environments such as financial regulatory agencies or law firms. Demonstrated knowledge of Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) requirements including the Banking Act, Securities and Futures Act, Financial Advisers Act, along with their practical application within business contexts. Excellent writing abilities paired with strong presentation skills that enable you to communicate effectively across a wide range of stakeholders situated in different countries. Meticulous attention to detail combined with analytical skills that allow you to assess complex regulatory information accurately while maintaining thorough documentation. Proven ability to work collaboratively within teams while also being dependable when delivering tasks independently under guidance from senior colleagues. Some understanding of core banking compliance areas as well as wholesale banking products and services is preferred for this position. Project management skills are considered advantageous for tracking multiple actions simultaneously within tight deadlines. Previous experience supporting compliance-related projects or participating in audits is desirable but not mandatory. We regret to inform that only shortlisted applicants will be notified. Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG) is an equal opportunity employer. We view our employees as our key assets as they are fundamental to our long-term growth and success. MUFG is committed to hiring based on merit and organsational fit, regardless of race, religion or gender. Our client is seeking an experienced AI Consultant to lead the end-to-end AI transformation of our business. Jobs People Learning Clear text Clear text Clear text Clear text Clear text Sign in Join now Analyst, Finance & Credit Life AIA Singapore Tampines, East Region, Singapore Apply Analyst, Finance & Credit Life AIA Singapore Tampines, East Region, Singapore 8 hours ago Over 200 applicants See who AIA Singapore has hired for this role Apply Save Report this job At AIA we’ve started an exciting movement to create a healthier, more sustainable future for everyone. It’s about finding new ways to not only better people's lives, but to better the communities and environments we live in. Encompassing our ambition of helping a billion people live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives by 2030. And to get there, we need ambitious people who believe in playing an important part in shaping that future. People seeking unmatched career and personal growth opportunities, who are driven to work with, and learn from some of the most inspiring and supportive leaders in the business. Sound like you? Then read on. About The Role This role is to contribute effectively to the Finance & Credit Life team by managing the end-to-end administration of Credit Life policies, including policy and system setup, member data maintenance, billing and payment processing, and customer communications - ensuring operational accuracy, compliance, and service excellence. What You’ll Be Doing Administer new and existing policies, including enrolments, renewals, and terminations. Maintain accurate member records and system databases, including changes to personal details and payment information. Configure policy setups with plan details, benefits, premiums, and individual billing. Activate system notifications for new policies and members. Prepare and distribute policy documents such as product summaries, certificates of insurance, and annual statements. Conduct User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for new policies and system enhancements. Process payments for new and existing members, including online transactions, GIRO setups, and recurring payment configurations. Liaise with banks, brokers, and customers on payment and policy-related matters. Respond promptly to enquiries via phone, email, and shared mailboxes. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure operational efficiency. Prepare and submit Monthly Business Reports (MBR) and Daily Business Reports (DBR). Manage premium collections and reconcile payment receipts accurately. Upload and manage policy documents in the imaging system. Ensure data integrity and compliance with standard operating procedures. Handle member feedback and resolve issues professionally across all communication channels. Provide administrative support and manage operational tasks, including billing arrangements. Support ad-hoc duties related to policy administration. Lead the development and management of tailored Term Life plans for Affinity group members to drive revenue growth and enhance customer experience in alignment with strategic goals. What We Are Looking For Possess a recognized university degree. Possess insurance knowledge is an advantage. Possess knowledge in MS Office (Excel, Words, and PowerPoint). Meticulous, detail-oriented, and good time management skills. Good interpersonal, communication and organizational skills. Willingness to work and learn in a fast-paced environment. Good team player and ability to work independently. Minimum 2 years relevant experience in insurance industry and in similar capacity Build a career with us as we help our customers and the community live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives. You must provide all requested information, including Personal Data, to be considered for this career opportunity. Failure to provide such information may influence the processing and outcome of your application. You are responsible for ensuring that the information you submit is accurate and up-to-date. ABOUT AIA For over a century, AIA has served the ever-changing needs of our customers across Asia-Pacific. Our Purpose to help millions of people live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives is at the heart of everything we do. As pioneering innovators, we’re now transforming AIA to be faster, simpler and more connected to create better solutions and impactful experiences for our customers and communities. AIA encourages and enables our people to act with clarity, courage and humanity in service of our Purpose. JOIN US At AIA, we believe in empowering every one of our people to find their 'better' - in the work they do, the career they build, the life they live and the difference they make. Whether it’s investing better wellbeing, inspiring better learning, building better relationships, or making a better impact on customers, a career at AIA will challenge you to find new ways to pursue your ‘better’. AIA is committed to building a vibrant, diverse, and inclusive workforce for all employees to thrive in. Join us if you believe in creating a better tomorrow! Position Responsibilities Elicit and model business requirements/user stories; using appropriate analysis techniques, understand and describe the business needs in a structure and level of detail that is usable by the particular stakeholders. Deliver through Agile methodologies; having experience working with Agile tools (e.g. Jira), participating in Sprints, backlog grooming and working with Product Owners for prioritization. Conduct business process re-engineering; using appropriate analysis techniques, understand and describe the as-is and to be processes whilst analyzing the impact on people, process and systems Validate requirements against business objectives; ensure that the requirements or designs deliver business value and directly support the business objectives and goals. Ensuring that we are using Human Centered Design principles to drive to this outcome. Define Business solution options; identify, explore and describe the solution (not architectural or technical) options that would achieve the business need. Analyze potential value and recommend solution; through a clear understanding of the business opportunity and objectives, assess the value that can be generated from the solution options and provide a recommendation to the key stakeholders. Customer focus; determine all requirements, solution designs and business value assessments from a customer perspective Focus on quality; ensure all outputs are of a high quality and consistency, presented through the defined templates, models and frameworks of the team. Testing; review and assist the business in test strategy, acceptance criteria development and execution of in-Sprint testing to ensure the solutions built meet business and product owner satisfaction (‘definition of done’) Transition and change; assist in the identification of the transition journey from current to target state, and the change management activities/focus areas to enable successful adoption of the solutions. Collaborative working; multi-cultural, multi-location teams, and cross functional working with teams from other business areas. Required Qualifications Degree holder, or equivalent, of Business Administration, Information Systems/Technology or related discipline Minimum 7 years of solid business analysis experiences in Financial Services (products, administration or distribution domain), ideally with specific Life Insurance or Bancassurance Experience in insurance distribution, operations or digital projects would be an advantage Practical experience in Agile delivery methods. Scrum Master or Product Owner experience would be desirable Strong analytical and creative problem solving skills, good communicator at all business levels, with the ability to exercise flexibility and judgment in a dynamic environment Good facilitation and influencing skills, including ability to work effectively across a large organization, developing strong cross–functional relationships Well organized and high attention to detail, with the ability to adapt to a fast-paced environment and changing priorities Strong PC skills including MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Visio When You Join Our Team We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want. We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words. As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
